channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: DBD Cornucopia is now available to play online!
item.description: In development, we are used to understanding threat modelling as a structured method to make applications and other software secure. And in this, “secure” usually means to protect against adverse...

item.title: Studiul Princeton pe 10.000 de căutări: de ce conținutul structurat dominează răspunsurile AI
item.description: Dacă lucrezi în SEO, probabil ai auzit că Google a eliminat FAQ rich results pe 7 mai 2026. Dar înainte să ștergi markup-ul de pe site, citește ce a găsit un studiu academic serios despre cum...

item.title: Building BetLogic with Next.js: AI Football Previews, Bet Slip Checks and Automated Content
item.description: I’m building BetLogic, an AI-powered football intelligence platform focused on match previews, risk-aware predictions, transparent tracking and football content automation. The idea started from a...

item.title: The difference between "this shouldn't happen" and "this cannot happen" in AI content pipelines
item.description: About three weeks ago I finished organizing something I had been avoiding for years. Two hundred and twenty-six files across my local drive, Google Drive, and a decade's worth of downloads. North...

item.title: Blind Signatures Explained: Getting Something Signed Without Revealing It
item.description: Imagine handing a notary a sealed envelope with carbon paper inside, having them stamp the outside, and walking away with their signature pressed onto a document they never read. That is a blind...

item.title: The boring pre-launch security check AI-built apps should run
item.description: AI builders make it wonderfully easy to get from idea to demo. Lovable can give you a SaaS-looking app in an afternoon. Bolt can wire together a prototype fast enough that your roadmap starts to...

item.title: My First article My Journey as a software engineer.....
item.description: It wasn't all rosy at first. I started off with no knowledge at all. The only thing I knew back then was that coding is used for building websites, games, and hacking. All I had was an undying...

item.title: From Mock API Workflow to Delivery-Ready Asset: Extending a Shopify-style Reporting Case Study
item.description: In the previous article, I introduced a public Shopify-style API reporting workflow case study. That article focused on one question: Can I show the workflow shape publicly without exposing...

item.title: DeepL acquires Mixhalo for live-event audio streaming and translation
item.description: With this acquisition, DeepL is opening an office in San Francisco to expand its U.S. business.

item.title: Why We Built “Ghiras”: Finding Quiet in a Distracted World
item.description: Why We Built “Ghiras”: Finding Quiet in a Distracted World In a time when every digital space fights for our attention, our phones have become a constant source of noise. The painful irony is that...

item.title: The Download: a reality check for geoengineering and the science of interoception
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hacking the atmosphere: geoengineering gets a reality...

item.title: AI image generation is easy. Building the handoff layer is harder.
item.description: AI image generation is easy. Building the handoff layer is harder. A few days ago, I launched and continued testing a small AI tattoo idea tool. At first, I thought the main challenge would be...

item.title: Will Matter finally be able to do what it should have always done?
item.description: Matter, the smart home interoperability standard, might finally get a feature that should have been there from day one: a single shared Matter network managed by multiple ecosystems. With this...

item.title: Final Fantasy meets Zelda? Yes, please
item.description: Let's be real: There's no shortage of Zelda-style games to play right now. That's true even if you've exhausted all there is to see in Tears of the Kingdom or Echoes of Wisdom. Indie developers...

item.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
item.description: For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain. The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must...

item.title: My backyard made me a color-changing smart lighting convert
item.description: I'll admit it. I was wrong. Wildly colorful lighting is delightful for your smart home - well, outdoors, at least. Smart lighting is one of my favorite features of the smart home - it combines...

item.title: Windows and Linux users: The deadline to update Secure Boot keys is near
item.description: What you need to know about the expiration of keys securing your machine's boot sequence.

item.title: AI Use by the US Government
item.description: On 14 April, the Trump administration quietly acknowledged the widespread use of AI to automate government processes. The office of management and budget (OMB) disclosed a staggering 3,611 active...

item.title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
item.description: Pinterest has launched 'Ask Pinterest,' an experimental AI-powered shopping app that lets users seek recommendations and inspiration through a conversational interface.

item.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
item.description: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. But when a vulnerability does drop — like MongoBleed earlier...

item.title: From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
The next humanoid robot might not look human at all
item.description: The next humanoid robot might not have a head. It might not have legs. It might even sit on a wheeled base and fold down like a deck chair. But, as Genesis AI puts it, "humanoid robots don't need...

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial...

item.title: GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks
Entrepreneurs in Nairobi make the case for going solar
item.description: Most of Kenya’s power grid runs on renewables. But with 25% of communities lacking centralized electricity, the nation is looking to off-grid solar to hit its goal of delivering universal...

item.title: Hacking the atmosphere: Geoengineering gets a reality check
item.description: Jim Franke pulls away the cover page of a presentation on the wraparound desk in his office, revealing an illustration of an odd-­looking aircraft with massive wings stretching out from a stubby...

item.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
item.description: As many as 144 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications,...

item.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited...

item.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
item.description: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

item.title: Year of free HPE software a “step in the correct direction” in VMware rivalry
item.description: Partner tells Ars that HPE should be giving out more free VM Essentials licenses.
